reviews.title: Very limited life span

Second vibration came with a short screen request to shut down the watch.  Side button quit working after about a month but was not an issue. reviews.title: Pretty good watch
reviews.text: This is my first "smart watch" so I'm still getting used to it.  The biggest user issue I have is that it isn't simply ON when I look at it.  It usually turns on if I move my arm properly, and I can switch it ON using my other hand.  But, when in busy or messy tasks, that's not the best thing.  The sleep tracker may or may not be really accurate....the step tracker is running at about 50% of my Fitbit pocket unit.  I'm thinking that the watch only really counts steps if my arm is free and swinging.  So, walking a half mile, pushing a shopping cart sounds as zero steps.  Pulse and blood oxygen seem to be close anyway.  But, the band is comfortable, and doesn't have that slimy silicone feel that some do.  Big numbers on the face and easy to read, and lots of faces available or put your own image up from your phone library.  Overall good value in my humble opinion.  Oh.....battery life is amazing.  Just charged it today because it was convenient.  But, it had been running on last week's charge, eight days, and was only down one half of the battery indicator.

reviews.title: Great watch, much better than my old "smart" watch
reviews.text:
I got Moto 360 several years ago when smart watches first came out and it was nice, but annoying. It was tied into everything and kept giving me alerts and so eventually I got tired of it. My wife also had a first generation Samsung Gear watch and that was about the same as the Moto.
Well, fast forward a few years and now my wife, daughter, son and daughter-in-law all have newer smart watches (Apple Watches and Fitbit) and they all love them. It was time to get a new phone, so I got a Samsung S20 FE and then decided to get the Samsung watch. I saw on the reviews that it really works well with Samsung phones and so I decided to get the Watch 3. I was looking at the Watch 2, which had a few less features and then I found the Watch 3 in the 41mm size and then found a refurbished one so I ended up paying about the same for the Watch 3 as I would have for the Watch 2.
I'm very impressed with the watch and really love it. I didn't get the one with a cell phone, it just connects to my phone with bluetooth and/or wifi, but it's really amazing. I get my messages, and can turn off the ones that I don't want and then I also get all the health apps. So, now it tells me to get off my chair and walk around and does EKG and stress tesing and Oxygen levels..
I was concerned that the 41 mm might be too small, but it is a great size for me as you can see from the pic. I have had a Citizen Echo Drive for more than 10 years and it is a little larger, but I like the size of the Watch 3 better.
If you are thinking about getting a watch, this one is a good deal right now and I believe it does work best if you use it with a Samsung phone.

reviews.title: It's Perfect!
reviews.text: What an amazing watch! My favorite color is red so when I saw this I just had to have it. This a Wear OS Google and that app helps this watch work seamlessly. Pairing is incredibly easy and takes only a few minutes. Once the watch is paired to the mobile phone it has all the capabilities the phone has. It shows all notifications, plays music and uses google assistance. Anything the Internet can do this watch can do. Google Fit tracks activities and bpm’s as well. There is a microphone on the watch so you can actually answer a call or ask google for anything. I love personalizing my stuff so I downloaded a free app off google play called Facer to choose one of their watch faces or make my own and of course my watch face is now my favorite show. This is in the settings just hit the display setting go all the way down the list of watch faces to the end and hit the button that says add more faces. This comes with a watch charger but not the USB adapter and a manual. The watch box is really nice. The best feature is when you turn your arm to see what time it is the home screen wakes up. It takes about 10 minutes to charge it which to me is super fast. It has a silicon strap and it's waterproof. I think the red metal is great and I am so happy with this perfect Michael Kors Smartwatch.
reviews.author: AFPFSWOQKMB23WJPUFIMHEH46BCA

reviews.title: It could have been so much better.
reviews.text: I have so many feelings about this watch. First off, set up was a breeze. You just open your app store and get the Google app directed. Pairing and synching up your info is very easy. The watch is very beautiful! I was scared the crystals would rub right off like glitter, but I have not had any issues so far. Now for all the bad stuff.. first and foremost the battery life is terrible. I would give it 9-10 hours max. I'm not much of an app person either, so if you are then that means your battery life will be even lower. It has its own charger, so that means you'll have to carry it around with you, especially big your planning to go out after work or school which is very inconvenient. I with it was a standard C USB charger so I can just use my phone charger, but sadly that's not the case. I did an experiment to see how long the battery would last. I charged it to 100%, and left it in my desk right before bed. In the morning at 8 am, I am at 15%. Whoa. Yeah, the battery is just really really terrible. So I thought I'd just change the battery settings..well there is only one other option and that is to go into battery saving mode where all it will display is the time. That in my opinion defeats the purpose of a smart watch. Other than that, I love how I can customize watch faces by using my social media, or pick from a pre made list by Michael Kors. You can also get email notifications, download apps like Google keep to take notes by speaking into your watch, as well as use Google pay, activity tracker, weather, and pause and play music without taking out your phone. So yeah, if your okay with a short battery life, then everything else about this watch is awesome. Just giving you the real tea. Hope you found this review helpful.

The screen colors are great! Vibrant colors and clear graphics. The touchscreen itself can lag just a little, but overall responds very well. Neither one of us have ever had a smartwatch before and I was a little apprehensive about setting it up, but I downloaded the wear OS app to her phone and from there, it just walked us through the process. She's now got it set up to check emails and can generally do anything on the internet that she would do with her phone. Then theres the added advantage of the fitness stuff... heart rate monitor, etc.

All in all, she loves it!

reviews.title: Still Searching
reviews.text:
The MKGO has the best battery life of any WearOS watches I've tried. On a fairly busy day with the app running in the background on my iPhone the whole time, I was able to wear the watch from 6 a.m. until I went to bed around 10. The other three android watches I've tried didn't make it to dinner.

But this doesn't have a speaker.

Why doesn't this have a speaker?

One of the indispensable features of my Apple watch is being able to answer a call quickly when I have stepped out of the room my phone is in. I have lupus. I can't run. People somehow seem to know to call about something important when I've gone to the bathroom or stepped out to the mailbox. I'm close enough to be connected to my phone, but not close enough to answer it.

This never happens with spam calls. Just the ones from the people I won't be able to get back to through their nightmare phone menus.

The lack of this feature makes the watch unsuitable for me, and that makes me sad, because the iPhone is a begware spamming disappointment machine these days, and I really want to move to android.

reviews.title: worth the try, gorgeous clear display, ease of use, fits my 7" wrist,
reviews.text:
It's pretty good, especially if you dont need a name brand. It has a beautifully clear display. Easy to install app and pairs well with no hassle. Has all the bells and whistles of name brand products. While I prefer fitbit due to familiarity.

I havent put it entirely through its paces. I noticed that it was registering nearly same heart rate as my fitbit sense. I havent it worn it long enough to determine blood oxygen. There is still a lot to learn. The learning curve feels a little clunky compared to my fitbit.

Has ability to connect to Alexa, take and make calls, music. Can make changes through app or scroll through the screen face.

The watch band fits my 7" wrist and feels comfortable enough. Typical silicone band. It is attached via pins, so I imagine it is possible to swap it out but not sure. I may try some old bands to see if it is doable. I'd also prefer for it to have a soft protective cover (bumper guard) to protect screen and edges.

I still prefer the ease of using a name brand, but this has a good looking interface. Main thing is to see how accurate it is. I think it has been fine for heart rate, steps, but will need more sleep time for seeing how well it tracks sleep and oxygen.

I dont rate on price, but I comment. This is pretty reasonable, but it looks like there are many similar products for less. So compared to a name brand of similar features this is a bargain. Compared to other no name generics, this is competitive. Some are easily $20 less. I noticed the price on this vacillates. I think I got it for $70, and have seen it as low as 35-40.

reviews.title: After a year and a half...can't wait to switch, Spotify is the worst part of this watch by far
reviews.text:
I have owned this watch for a year and a half now. I use it almost daily for my walks/runs, weight workouts, and indoor bike workouts. The main reason I chose this watch in the first place was because it was one of the smaller ones available. I am 5'9 but have small wrists, and really disliked the 'utilitarian' look of larger watches. After a year and a half, I am so tired of the issues with it and ready to jump ship. If I used this watch more regularly (I only wear it for workouts - not all day), I would have switched a LONG time ago. The amount of people who have complained about the same issues for YEARS without Garmin doing anything makes me hesitant to purchase another Garmin product, especially with the Apple Watch, Samsung, and now Google buying out Fitbit and coming up with a watch.
- Spotify on this watch seriously sucks. If you check Spotify forums, you will find COUNTLESS threads of suggestions for the Spotify app on this watch that should truly be basic features at this price. Do they implement them or listen? No. Also, there is no way to contact Spotify customer service - you just get pointed towards their 'community forum' where you have people complaining of the same exact things with no fix. You can't skip forward on any track, at all. I listen to a lot of podcasts when I walk. Some are 2 hours in length while my walk is only 1 hour. This watch constantly either reverts to the very beginning of my podcast so I have to start all over (because again, you can't skip forward - so have fun listening to ads as well!) in anything, only updates 1 song out of 15 on my playlist or get the error 'sync failed', or some other random issue. Also, if you have a playlist on Spotify, you cannot choose the track you want to listen to. You have to hit the >> button to get to where you want. Really? I hope Garmin drops Spotify because they have been a truly horrible app on this watch the entire time with no effort to make updates. Take a look at the reviews of Spotify on the Garmin store if you don't believe me.
- Battery life is a couple of days. However, there are points in time where my watch will be at say 80% the night before, I get ready to leave and go my walk the next morning, pick up my watch, and it's at 8%. WTF? This happens enough to be super annoying and apparently is not an 'only me' problem. It appears to be tied to when the watch updates.
- I tried to wear this to track my sleep in the beginning and gave up after two nights due to the extreme disco effect of the 'gesture' motions. I tried turning on the 'do not disturb' function advertised on the watch which is supposed to disable this stupidly bright light that WILL wake you and whoever is next to you up. Didn't work. Do a search for 'Garmin watch lights up in middle of the night' and you will find all sorts of people complaining of the same thing, and not just on this version of the watch.
- GPS has issues connecting and can take several minutes which is pretty annoying when you try to start tracking an activity only to see a red bar when trying to connect. I 'sync' my watch to my phone constantly so that is not the issue. I have switched between the GPS options Garmin offers on the watch to no avail. Search for this issue as well and you will see lots of people with the same complaint.
reviews.author: AFAY3OIVXMQFSAHFG6E66DHHE35Q

reviews.title: Battery life is horrible!!!
reviews.text:
I charged new watch out of the box to 100%. Turned off all connections to make a clean test. Put the watch on the table and did not touch it. In about 23 hours the charge was 20% without actively using the watch at all or turning on the screen. It is absolutely horrible. Dormant watch consumed 80% of power.

I am wearing Huawei 6 band for a week and it is still running on the same charge, it's around 15% now. It has the same sport and health functions like Samsung watch and I paid $40 for it. It is also not cumbersome like Samsung watch.

Samsung needs to fix their battery life. Thinking about return.

ECG tracker stopped working. When I try to run it on the watch , it says "New version is available. Install the update from Galaxy App Store to continue using the app. If I click on the Update button on the watch, I get "Page not found" on the phone browser.

I've been through several support chats, calls, sms, they tried to remote to my phone! and still the issue with the watch was not solved. Every support team was trying to send me somewhere else. I spent several hours talking to Samsung support.

The issue with this watch is that it is using Samsung's own home brewed half-baked operating system. It is clunky. It is misleading. If you opened some app like browser on the watch, there is no way to close it. It is running draining power. I was able to resolve the ECG app issue on yhe watch by reinstalling Galaxy Wear app on my phone. It was just a wild guess. The watch tips and Samsung support troubleshooting was absolutely useless. This watch is a bad news.
reviews.author: AHNOSMQ6G77VJP56MWY37AZT5RNA

reviews.title: Everything matched my expectations
reviews.text:
I use it for cycling and running (jogging really). It provides some nice analyses. The running distance is sometimes high and sometimes low. The biking distance is always a little low, about 1/4 mile out of 15 total. I have no other way of checking my heart rate during exercise but it shoots up to as high as 165. Resting it's 55. It does a good job of measuring sleep. It reports awake time, REM, light sleep, and deep sleep along with a score and oxygen. When I get a high score, I also feel more rested.

I charge it when I shower, so once a day. Takes a long time, almost 2 hours. Would be nice if it charged quicker. My showers aren't that long.

The watch faces are customizable. For some reason the different display options are called complications. You can choose from temperature, heartbeat, battery level, compass, exercise, etc. The watch face I use (Info Board) has 5 complications along with time, day, and 4 hour temperature forecast.
reviews.author: AENRK7JSJWICWF2QYBDWBIGVLBDA

reviews.title: Comfortable and attractive
reviews.text:
These bands are comfortable. That was my main hope, and the reason I bought a fabric band, and I was not disappointed. It doesn't leave me with a sweaty or chafed wrist, and it doesn't have metal links that have to fit together perfectly to keep the watch on. I like the heathery look, because it's just the right about of casual without looking cheap or tacky, and there is no paint to chip off.

The bands have a belt-style buckle, with a lot of holes for size adjustment. I got the large size, and I count seven bigger and four smaller than my wrist. I have a slightly larger than average wrist for a woman, possibly more like small-to-average for a man. So I would say the sizing is pretty forgiving. reviews.title: Most under-rated brand
reviews.text:
I'm a big fan of the Mi Band series of trackers, and this is the first device that finally got me to switch to something else. If you aren't familiar, Xiaomi and Huami aren't big brands in the US compared to other countries, but these are solid high quality products and not your standard fare Chinese junk. I've tried a LOT of different fitness trackers and smart watches- Everything from Google WearOS, Fitbit, Apple, and all the way down to the cheap trackers from Chinese throwaway brands with obscure apps. For a long time I've said I liked the features and quality of the expensive brands, but found they all had major flaws (battery life being a major one- I don't want another thing to charge as often as a phone!). The Mi band from Xiaomi was super affordable and high quality compared to others in that price range, and had a battery that only required being charged once a month.
This is basically the smarter older brother of that band. It is compatible with Mi Fit, so all my data transferred right over, but it is so much nicer than the band. For starters, the notification system is easier to read and keep up with messages, but the benefits go far beyond a larger screen. The music control is a nice touch, I like being able to pause and adjust the volume without taking my phone out of my pocket if playing on a BT speaker while I'm doing dishes, for example. The tracking is pretty accurate compared to other expensive brands, and while it doesn't support apps like Wear OS and Apple Watches, it has all the fitness functions you'd need built in already, including some fun widgets beyond the expected ones like a stopwatch, phone finder and even a compass! And unlike cheap brands, the UI is responsive and smooth. Not the smoothest out there for sure, but more than acceptably smooth considering how much a flagship smartwatch costs compared to this.
It has support for voice commands, but I honestly found that feature kind of gimmicky more than useful. The higher end version of this includes a speaker which might make the command function more useful as sort of a digital assistant, not to mention the ability to take phone calls from your wrist. But the model I got to try out lacks the speaker, which is a fine compromise for a cheaper price tag all things considered. The battery life at first was just OK, a few days on a charge if using lift-to-wake functions, but just as on my Mi Band tracker of old, I found if I turn off auto-wake and rely on using the button to wake the display, the battery life goes from counting in days to counting in weeks. That's the killer feature of this device- the ability to leave it on to track fitness and sleep habits without having to charge it so often. I'll have to charge it more than my old Mi Band's monthly top up for sure, probably at least 2x a month, but that's still very manageable.
So that's it, after years of loving my Mi Bands and considering it the standard by which all practical fitness trackers should be judged, I'm finally moving on to an Amazfit watch. This is a difficult value to beat.

reviews.title: Best Wear OS for true Android users, but not perfect
reviews.text:
I will avoid the facts that this watch is outdated at launch by its hardware (processor). That, coupled with retail and even discounted price also are out of step with the competition. These two big gripes aside. The watch is good and likely the best choice going into 2023 for pure Android Wear OS users (not looking at Apple or Tizen overlay users.)

Its not perfect and Pixel users will benefit the most when paired to this watch. Its lost most of the lag recent watches from other Wear OS brands. Has all the sensors the majority of buyers could want based on watch tech right now. It swipes, it plays well with the apps made for it. It gives you the info you ask of it. There are even a few nice 3rd part accessories now to avoid Google Gouging for proprietary bands. And there are few quirks I hope they iron out soon. Mainly, when the phone is in Bedtime Mode. Make the watch do it too, or add that Zen Mode feature to the watch also. It sucks to have animals tripping the security cameras outside dinging on my wrist and killing my sleep score. The snooze/do not disturb button on watch fails too easily and you have turn it on/off manually everytime.

As many claim, the battery sucks. Sort of. I can go up to 36+ hours on a slow day, and many others can too. It will depend on too many factors to list. But basically your popularity with notifications and thinking you need the screen always on are the biggest battery hurdles. Most can get use to touch to wake. And maybe only having text, phone, front door doorbell and one email be allowed to bother your watch with notifications. Do you really need to know what cousin Billy had at Dairy Queen when he posts the same pic/vid on Facebook, Instagram, Tic Toc and YouTube, etc. Find a nice balance of wrist information along with what sensors you need checking if you are still alive. You can start the day and track everything till the next morning when you charge it again while you shower and get the next day started. It charges quickly. And most other Wear OS watches have the same daily charge requirements. Nobody on Wear OS is winning on battery. Or Turn every setting all the way up and have a spare charger puck ready for a mid day juice. If you go that route. I suggest a quick release band to ease the removal of the watch.

So, its not up to the level or price of the Ultra Apple Watch. And if you don't care for Samsung. Your love for stock Android will eventually lead you back to this watch. Unless you forgo ECG sensors. The price will drop soon. Cases are minimizing the fragility of an ALL glass jelly bean design. But when comes to keeping your phone pocketed and yet still have info relayed in a useful way. Pixel Watch is the best current albeit not perfect solution for now.
reviews.author: AED5VKZVSF7PC3TOET3PKBA5J2JA

reviews.title: Would be the perfect watch if the battery was replaceable
reviews.text:
I normally don't like smart watches because I hate the idea of spending hundreds of dollars on something with a non-replaceable battery that will wear out in a few years rendering the watch useless. Even though I do love my new Instinct 2 watch, I'm still not crazy about the wasteful business model of smart watches, and am not sure I will buy another after this one. That said, the Instinct 2 is a near perfect watch in almost every way... it is feature packed, fully customizable (without being over the top), has a long battery life, and is very comfortable as a daily driver watch.

I use mine for the usual day-to-day watch stuff, and hiking primarily. I don't use any of the smart phone or health features (heart rate, pulse ox, etc.) as I don't care about them. I use the compass, altimeter, and gps tracking features frequently, and they all work great.

Overall, this is a fantastic watch for what it is. However, I really, REALLY hate that it has a non-replaceable battery that will inevitably wear out after only a few years. I know that this is the case as I had a Fenix 5 before this one that lasted about 4 years before the battery began dying quickly. It essentially became a $500 door stop after that...

reviews.title: I like this one better than my other smart watch
reviews.text: I have a big deal designer's name brand smart watch which has a battery life of like 12 hours max and that's with disabling just about everything!  The problem with charging that (and this) smart watch is the fact that you can't just plug them in anywhere with a regular micro usb.  They have their proprietary charging "cradle" that gloms onto the back of the watch.  This one seem so far to have a higher battery life without having to shut down every app to extend the battery life.  The only thing I would wish is that if they market these to both men and women, MAKE THE WATCH FACE A BIT SMALLER!!!  I understand about seeing/reading everything on the watch, but JEEZ it is HUGE!!!  I had to fasten it further down on my wrist/arm so the watch face fit my arm.  I'm not a tiny woman nor am I a big one, so if that's a problem for an average woman, please take note and scale it back by about 3%.  Otherwise, this is a great smart watch.  Runs on Google OS which my other watch runs on and seems to do pretty much the same things.

reviews.title: Luxury, time and tracking for the seasoned athlete or outdoorsman!
reviews.text:
First things first: you have to be a true athlete or outdoorsman to really want to spend this kind of money on a smartwatch when there are others, but the features on this one make it exceptional. My son has truly been able to maintain his sanity after having to leave school and fly solo during this crisis. He has thrown himself into a variety of workouts to keep himself busy since he can't socialize and thinks this watch is one of the best items he owns.

The GPS features allow him to explore routes, trails, and areas he wasn't previously acquainted with (offline too!) and the health data points are accurate. There are dozens of sports modes for whatever activity he chooses for the day and the display itself is beautiful, bright and very responsive.

I wouldn't pay this much for a watch because I prefer a sedentary lifestyle (LOL) but for the athlete in your family with a long and hot summer ahead, this is the perfect graduation gift too!

If you are not COMPLETELY satisfied with the Versa 3/ Sense mesh metal band, just click "Contact Seller" for quick service

reviews.title: Nice Band
reviews.text: I like having options when it comes to the band of my Fitbit Versa3. This one looks nice. It's a little less casual than the silicone ones. It fits my Versa fine and was easy to attach. I wear my Fitbit on my left arm (I'm right handed). I found it a little more difficult to put on my wrist than my other bands. I was able to get it, but it took a few tries. Once on my wrist, my Versa feels secure and I'm not concerned about it coming undone.
reviews.author: AHV6QCNBJNSGLATP56JAWJ3C4G2A
